license_note: MiroFish upstream is AGPL-3.0. This project keeps a clean-room local planning adapter and does not vendor upstream code.
purpose: MiroFish-style swarm simulation planning for market, public-opinion, policy, and scenario prediction research.
workflow: Seed extraction and source reliability scoring
Entity, relationship, and memory graph planning
Parallel swarm simulation rounds with temporal memory updates
Scenario branch comparison and contradiction logging
Prediction report generation with confidence, assumptions, and indicators to monitor

agent_cohorts.description: Models CLOB liquidity, spread, queue behavior, and short-horizon positioning.
Models social/news reaction, narrative momentum, and attention decay.
Models policy drafts, regulatory shocks, and institutional constraints.
Looks for manipulation, false signals, overfitting, and tail-risk paths.
Aggregates post-simulation evidence into a compact prediction report.

persona_prompt_registry.integration_rule: Use MiroFish personas for bounded simulation planning only. Final trading recommendations must be reviewed by LLM Council and deterministic Lobstar guardrails.

guardrails: Do not send private keys, Telegram tokens, Vault tokens, raw logs, or unredacted credentials into simulations.
Use simulation output as research only; do not execute trades from it directly.
Require deterministic parser, risk engine, ledger validation, and execution mode checks before any trading action.
For finance scenarios, include fees, slippage, latency, liquidity, and adversarial manipulation assumptions.
Keep round and agent counts bounded to avoid runaway token usage.
